\makeatletter
\def\@nodottedtocline#1#2#3#4#5{%
    \vskip \z@ \@plus.2\p@
    {\leftskip #2\relax \rightskip \@tocrmarg \parfillskip -\rightskip
     \parindent #2\relax\@afterindenttrue
     \interlinepenalty\@M
     \leavevmode
     \@tempdima #3\relax
     \advance\leftskip \@tempdima \null\nobreak\hskip -\leftskip
     {#4}\hfill\null\par}}
\newcommand*\l@heading{\@nodottedtocline{0}{0em}{0em}}
\makeatother
\newcommand\headinginfo[1]{%
  \addcontentsline{lot}{heading}{{#1}}%
  \addtocontents{lot}{\vspace{5pt}}}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 12 & \footnotesize rf\_blk3\_remap\-\_offset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  block 3 remap offset when the block 3 remap function is enabled. \newline
The address after remap will be original addr + rf\_blk3\_remap\_offset \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{15.17 CACHE\_CFG0}{anchor30}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{15.17 CACHE\_CFG0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{15.17 CACHE\_CFG0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000040}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_write\_mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  cache write operation mode: \newline
2’b00: write through\newline
2’b01: write back, no write allocate\newline
2’b10: write back, write allocate \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 2 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_byp & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Bit [5]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[3] from CM4 go through cache  controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[3] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [4]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[2] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[2] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [3]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[1] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[1] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [2]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[0] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[0] is provided by cache controller register \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 6 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_bus\_\-reg & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache bus AHB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 10 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_cach\-e\_reg & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xB & \footnotesize  hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache ctrl AHB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize ahb\_ram\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Cache Data Ram Can be Write/Read Throught rf AHB Bus \\
\hline
\footnotesize 29 : 28 & \footnotesize rf\_cache\_size\-\_sel & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  cache size selection: \newline
0: 4K Byte\newline
1: 8K Byte\newline
2: 16K Byte\newline
3: 32K Byte \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_exec\_\-mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: (recommended) software can run in cacheable region during software command processing\newline
1: software cannot run in cacheable region during software command processing \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize rf\_debug\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  cache debug mode enable: \newline
0: normal mode\newline
1: debug mode\newline
This bit MUST always be cleared during cache operating \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{15.18 BUS\_STS0}{anchor31}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{15.18 BUS\_STS0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{15.18 BUS\_STS0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000044}\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\pdfbookmark[2]{15.19 CACHE\_STS0}{anchor32}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{15.19 CACHE\_STS0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{15.19 CACHE\_STS0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000048}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_cache\_st & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_cache\_st \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_st & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_cmd\_st \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rf\_write\_ongo\-ing & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_write\_ongoing, this is a status register for write through mode to avoid potential coherence issue.\newline
1’b1: the cache is still doing AHB write transaction to the main memory and the data is not written into the main memory.\newline
1’b0: the cache has finished AHB write transaction and the data is written into the main memory. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

cmd\_range : start address\newline
cmd\_entry  : entry address \\
\hline

cmd\_range : end address\newline
cmd\_entry  : not used \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{15.22 CMD\_CFG2}{anchor35}

\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{15.22 CMD\_CFG2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000058}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_type & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  software command type:\newline
6’h0 : clean all\newline
6’h1 : clean range\newline
6’h2 : clean entry\newline
6’h3 : reserved\newline
6’h4 : invalid all\newline
6’h5 : invalid range\newline
6’h6 : invalid entry\newline
6’h7 : reserved\newline
6’h8 : clean and invalid all\newline
6’h9 : clean and invalid range\newline
6’hA : clean and invalid entry\newline
6’hB : reserved \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_str & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  software command start: \newline
write 1 to this bit to issue one command \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{dma\_cx}
\section{dma\_cx}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{16.1 DMA\_PAUSE}{anchor46}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.1 DMA\_PAUSE}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.1 DMA\_PAUSE} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40020000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_PAUSE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Active High, pause all the channels.\newline
Note: If "DMA\_PAUSE" asserted active when the DMA  is busy, DMA would finish the current fragment before enter Pause State. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize DMA\_PAUSE\_STA\-TUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA Pause status.\newline
0 : In active state.\newline
1 : In Pause state. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.2 DMA\_FRAG\_WAIT}

\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_FRAG\_WAIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Wait Time between two successive Fragment. With unit of AHB clock. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize O\_ENDIAN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA output endian, \newline
0- little endian  \newline
1- big endian \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_INT\_RAW\_\-STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA 32 Channels Interrupt Raw Status.\newline
0 : No Interrupt need to be served.\newline
1 : Interrupt need to be served. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{16.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS}{anchor54}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40020020}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize DEST\_FSM\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Side FSM status.\newline
4'd0: IDLE\newline
4'd1: calculate burst parameter\newline
4'd2: calculate data number of burst\newline
4'd3: wait enough data buffer space for write  burst\newline
4'd4: start write burst\newline
4'd5: write burst execute\newline
4'd6: wait fragment done or limit outstanding  number\newline
4'd7: fragment write done \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 4 & \footnotesize SRC\_FSM\_STATU\-S & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Side FSM status.\newline
4'd0: IDLE\newline
4'd1: calculate burst parameter\newline
4'd2: calculate data number of burst\newline
4'd3: wait enough data buffer space for read  burst\newline
4'd4: start read burst\newline
4'd5: read burst execute\newline
4'd6: wait fragment done or limit outstanding  number\newline
4'd7: fragment read done \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize MAIN\_FSM\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  MAIN FSM status.\newline
5'd0: IDLE\newline
5'd1: load link list pointer\newline
5'd2: load link list source and dest address\newline
5'd3: load link list fragment and block length\newline
5'd4: load link list transaction length and  src/dest transfer step\newline
5'd5: load link list wrap\_ptr and wrap\_to\newline
5'd6: load full mode source and dest address\newline
5'd7: load full mode fragment and block length\newline
5'd8: load full mode transaction length and  src/dest transfer step\newline
5'd9: load full mode wrap\_ptr and wrap\_to\newline
5'd10: update block length\newline
5'd11: update transaction length\newline
5'd12: fragment execute\newline
5'd13: load list pointer and use src/dest fragment  step\newline
5'd14: load src/dest block step\newline
5'd15: load link list high 4bits pointer and  src/dest fragment step\newline
5'd16: load src/dest block step\newline
5'd17: transaction or link list done\newline
5'd18: update wrap info. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize HREADY\_DMAR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  dma read hready \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize HREADY\_DMAW & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  dma write hready \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize DMA\_BUSY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA Busy Status. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

bit0: In data byte receive\newline
bit1: In data bit receive\newline
bit2: Out data byte receive\newline
bit3: Out data bit receive \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize CRC\_GEN\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  CRC generator size \newline

2'b10:crc24 2'b11:crc32 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 8 & \footnotesize CRC\_INIT\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CRC initial mode\newline

\hline
\footnotesize 20 : 16 & \footnotesize CRC\_CH\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  DMA channel number for CRC check \\

\hline

\hline

\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{16.46.1 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE}{anchor92}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.46.1 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_PAUSE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Pause Enable.\newline
0 : Channel in active mode.\newline
1 : Channel pause.\newline
Active high, only pause the channel. If the  CHN\_PAUSE asserted when the channel is busy, it should be paused after the  current fragment finished. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize CHN\_PAUSE\_STA\-TUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Pause status. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{16.46.2 FULL\_CHN\_REQ}{anchor93}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_REQ}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.46.2 FULL\_CHN\_REQ} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_REQ & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Software DMA Request. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize CHN\_EXREQ\_MOD\-E & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel External DMA Request Mode: \newline
1- level, 0- edge \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize CHN\_EXREQ\_LEV\-EL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Channel External DMA Valid Level: \newline
1- high, 0- low \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{16.46.3 FULL\_CHN\_CFG}{anchor94}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_CFG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.46.3 FULL\_CHN\_CFG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021008}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Enable:\newline
0 : Disable the channel.\newline
1 : Enable the channel. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize LLIST\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list Enable, only available for full DMA  channel.\newline
0 : Disable the link list.\newline
1 : Enable the link list. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize LLIST\_CFG\_VAL\-ID & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  If "LLIST\_EN" asserted, active high indicates  Link list configure valid.\newline
0 : Link list configure in-valid. DMA controller  need obtain link list configure information by link list pointer.\newline
1 : link list configure information valid. DMA  controller would continue to complete the node and clear this bit.\newline
Note: Strongly recommend to observe only.  Carefully writing to this bit, which may cause DMA crashed. If had to do so,  pause this channel before changing. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize CHN\_PRIORITY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Request Priority. Four level of Priority  supported as list :\newline
2'b00 : Lowest priority.\newline
2'b01 : Secondary lowest priority.\newline
2'b10 : Tertiary lowest priority.\newline
2'b11 : Highest priority. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 17 : 16 & \footnotesize AHB\_BURST\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  AMBA Burst Select.\newline
2'b00 : INCR8;\newline
2'b01 : INCR4; \newline
2'b10 : INCR16; \newline
2'b11 : INCR16; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 23 : 20 & \footnotesize ERR\_TYPE\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Error Configure , which would be  cleared when "ERR\_INT\_STATUS" cleared:\newline
4'b0001 : Addr Wrap Error Configure;\newline
4'b0010 : Request Mode Error Configure;\newline
4'b0100 : Length Error Configure;\newline
4'b1000 :Unalign Error Configure;\newline
1 gt;  Addr wrap error configure\newline
Set  wrap enable in standard DMA channel.\newline
2 gt;  Request mode error configure\newline
Req\_mode==2  or 3 in in standard DMA channel.\newline
3 gt;  Length Error Configure\newline
d gt;  Frag\_len==0;\newline
e gt;  Blk\_len==0;\newline
f gt;  FULL DMA channel,trsc\_len==0;\newline
4 gt;  Unalign Error Configure\newline
c gt;  Src unsigned error\newline
a1 gt;  address unalign in halfword width\newline
error=(src\_data\_size[1:0]==1) amp; amp;(src\_addr[0] =0)\newline
a2 gt;  address unalign and trsf\_step unalign in word width\newline
error=(src\_data\_size[1:0]==2) amp; amp;(src\_addr[1:0] =0)\newline
amp; amp;(src\_trsf\_step =4)\newline
d gt;  dest unsigned error\newline
b1 gt;  address unalign in halfword width\newline
error=(dst\_data\_size[1:0]==1) amp; amp;(dst\_addr[0] =0)\newline
b2 gt;  address unalign and trsf\_step unalign in word width\newline
error=(dst\_data\_size[1:0]==2) amp; amp;(dst\_addr[1:0] =0)\newline
amp; amp;(dst\_trsf\_step =4) \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{16.46.4 FULL\_CHN\_INT}{anchor95}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_INT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.46.4 FULL\_CHN\_INT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4002100C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done interrupt enable. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt enable. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transaction done interrupt enable \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list done interrupt enable \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error interrupt enable. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done Raw interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Transaction interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Link List interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error raw interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Fragment Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Block Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 18 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Transaction Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Link list Masked interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error masked interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 24 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 25 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 26 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transaction done interrupt clear \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 27 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list done interrupt clear \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}

\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{16.46.7 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN}{anchor98}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{16.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{16.46.7 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021018}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 : 0 & \footnotesize FRG\_LEN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment Length, with byte unit.  Max 128K-1 bytes \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize BLK\_LEN\_REC\_\-H & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block counter[16:15], update after each fragment  done;\newline
Note: Normally configured to 2'b0. Only to be  configured other value during state retention after wake up \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize LLIST\_END & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list end flag. Active only when "LLIST\_EN"  asserted. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize SRC\_ADDR\_FIX\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Address Fix Enable:\newline
0 : Disable Source Address Fix mode.\newline
1 : Enable Source Address Fix mode.\newline
Active High would make source address fixed within the  fragment, while "FRAG\_STEP" and "BLK\_STEP" would be applied to address after  fragment done; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize DEST\_ADDR\_FIX\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Selection of the side using Address Fix mode.\newline
0 : Disable Destination Address Fix mode.\newline
1 : Enable Destination Address Fix mode.\newline
Active High would make destination address fixed within the  fragment, while "FRAG\_STEP" and "BLK\_STEP" would be applied to address after  fragment done; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 22 & \footnotesize ADDR\_WRAP\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Active High enable Address-wrapping for ring  buffer. The next address of DMA jumps to "WRAP\_START\_ADDR" address when the  current address matches "WRAP\_END\_ADDR" address:\newline
0 : Disable address wrap mode.\newline
1 : Enable address wrap mode. "WRAP\_START\_ADDR"  and "WRAP\_END\_ADDR" would be used to limit the address space of either Source  or Destination. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 23 & \footnotesize ADDR\_WRAP\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Selection of the side using Address wrap mode.\newline
0 : Address wrap on Source.\newline
1 : Address wrap on Destination.\newline
Only one side of a DMA channel can activate  address-wrapping mode at a time. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 25 : 24 & \footnotesize REQ\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request mode, For Full DMA channel:\newline
2'b00 : Fragment;\newline
2'b01 : Block;\newline
2'b10 : Transaction;\newline
2'b11 : Link List; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 27 : 26 & \footnotesize SWT\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Switch mode selection.\newline
2'b00 : ABCD = gt; ABCD;\newline
2'b01 : ABCD = gt; DCBA\newline
2'b10 : ABCD = gt; BADC;\newline
2'b11 : ABCD = gt; CDAB \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 29 : 28 & \footnotesize DEST\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination AHB Size.\newline
2'b00 : Byte;\newline
2'b01 : Half Word\newline
2'b10 : Word\newline
2'b11 : DWord. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 31 : 30 & \footnotesize SRC\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source AHB Size.\newline
2'b00 : Byte;\newline
2'b01 : Half Word\newline
2'b10 : Word\newline
2'b11 : DWord. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{uart0}
\section{uart0}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}{anchor194}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rbr\_thr\_dll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  receiver buffer when read, transmit holding register when write(In fifo mode, the written data will push to tx fifo).\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (low) bit [7:0]. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.2 IER\_DLH}{anchor195}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.2 IER\_DLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.2 IER\_DLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize erbfi\_dlh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx data available interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize etbei\_dlh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Transmit Holding Register Empty interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize elsi\_dlh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx Line Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 2. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize edssi\_dlh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Modem Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 3. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize dlh\_6\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  reserved;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit [6:4]. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_dlh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Programmable THRE interrupt mode enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 7. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.3 IIR\_FCR}{anchor196}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.3 IIR\_FCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.3 IIR\_FCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize iid\_0\_fifoe & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 0; Write:  fifo enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize iid\_1\_rfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 1; Write:  rx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize iid\_2\_xfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 2; Write:  tx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize iid\_3\_dmam & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 3; Write:  dma mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize tet & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx Empty Trigger. 0: fifo empty. 1: 2 characters in the fifo. 2: fifo 1/4 full. 3: fifo 1/2 full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize fifose\_rcvr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx trigger. 0: 1 character in the fifo. 1: fifo 1/4 full. 2: fifo 1/2 full. 3: fifo 2 less than full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.4 LCR}{anchor197}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.4 LCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.4 LCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003200C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize dls & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data length select. 0: 5bit, 1: 6bit, 2: 7bit, 3:8bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize stop & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of stop bits. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize pen & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize eps & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Even Parity select. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize stick\_parity & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Stick parity. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize bc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break control bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dlab & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Divisor latch access bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.5 MCR}{anchor198}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.5 MCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.5 MCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dtr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data terminal ready. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize rts & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request to send. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize out1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT1. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output1 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize out2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT2.  This is used to directly control the user-designated Output2 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize lb & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Loopback mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize afce & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Auto flow control enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize sire & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SIR mode enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.6 LSR}{anchor199}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.6 LSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.6 LSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data ready bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize oe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Overrun error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize pe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize fe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Framing Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize bi & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break interrupt bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize thre & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter holding register empty bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize temt & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter empty bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receiver fifo error bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.7 MSR}{anchor200}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.7 MSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.7 MSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dcts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta clear to send. This is used to indicate that the modem control line cts\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ddsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data set ready. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dsr\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize teri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Trailing edge of ring indicator. This is used to indicate that a change on the input ri\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[2](Out1) has changed state from a high to low. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ddcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data carrier detect. This is used to indicate that a change on line dcd\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[3](Out1) has changed. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize cts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Clear to send. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line cts\_n. \newline
0: cts\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: cts\_n input is asserted(logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data set ready. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dsr\_n. \newline
0: dsr\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dsr\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize ri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Ring indicator. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line ri\_n. \newline
0: ri\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: ri\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[2](Out1). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dcd\_n. \newline
0: dcd\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dcd\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[3](Out2). \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.8 SCR}{anchor201}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.8 SCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.8 SCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003201C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize scr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is for programmers to use as a temporary storage space. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.9 LPDLL}{anchor202}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.9 LPDLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.9 LPDLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the lower 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.10 LPDLH}{anchor203}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.10 LPDLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.10 LPDLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdlh & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the upper 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.11 FAR}{anchor204}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.11 FAR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.11 FAR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032070}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize fa & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  FIFO access regiseter. 1: FIFO access mode is enabled. 0: disabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.12 TFR}{anchor205}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.12 TFR}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.12 TFR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032074}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO read. \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.13 RFW}{anchor206}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.13 RFW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.13 RFW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40032078}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfwd & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo write data. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize rfpe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo parity error. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize rffe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo framing error. \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{19.14 USR}{anchor207}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.14 USR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.14 USR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003207C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: uart is idle or inactive; 1: uart is busy. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tfnf & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO not full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize rfne & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO not empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize rff & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{19.16 RFL}{anchor209}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.16 RFL}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfl & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This indicates the number of data entries in the receive fifo. \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{19.17 HTX}{anchor210}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.17 HTX}

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.17 HTX} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x400320A4}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize htx & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to halt transmissions for testing, so that the transmit fifo can be filled by the master. 0: halt tx disabled; 1: halt tx enabled. \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{19.18 DMASA}{anchor211}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{19.18 DMASA}

\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dmasa & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to perform a DMA software acknowledge if a transfer needs to be terminated due to an error condition. For example, if the DMA disables the channel, then the DW\_apb\_uart should clear its request. This cause the tx request, tx single, rx request and rx signle signals to de-assert.  This bit is 'self-clearing' \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{uart1}
\section{uart1}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}{anchor214}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rbr\_thr\_dll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  receiver buffer when read, transmit holding register when write(In fifo mode, the written data will push to tx fifo).\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (low) bit [7:0]. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.2 IER\_DLH}{anchor215}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.2 IER\_DLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.2 IER\_DLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize erbfi\_dlh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx data available interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize etbei\_dlh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Transmit Holding Register Empty interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize elsi\_dlh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx Line Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 2. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize edssi\_dlh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Modem Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 3. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize dlh\_6\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  reserved;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit [6:4]. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_dlh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Programmable THRE interrupt mode enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 7. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.3 IIR\_FCR}{anchor216}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.3 IIR\_FCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.3 IIR\_FCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize iid\_0\_fifoe & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 0; Write:  fifo enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize iid\_1\_rfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 1; Write:  rx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize iid\_2\_xfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 2; Write:  tx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize iid\_3\_dmam & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 3; Write:  dma mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize tet & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx Empty Trigger. 0: fifo empty. 1: 2 characters in the fifo. 2: fifo 1/4 full. 3: fifo 1/2 full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize fifose\_rcvr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx trigger. 0: 1 character in the fifo. 1: fifo 1/4 full. 2: fifo 1/2 full. 3: fifo 2 less than full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.4 LCR}{anchor217}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.4 LCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003300C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize dls & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data length select. 0: 5bit, 1: 6bit, 2: 7bit, 3:8bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize stop & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of stop bits. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize pen & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize eps & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Even Parity select. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize stick\_parity & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Stick parity. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize bc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break control bit. \\
\hline

\hline

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.5 MCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dtr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data terminal ready. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize rts & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request to send. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize out1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT1. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output1 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize out2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT2.  This is used to directly control the user-designated Output2 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize lb & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Loopback mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize afce & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Auto flow control enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize sire & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SIR mode enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.6 LSR}{anchor219}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.6 LSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.6 LSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data ready bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize oe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Overrun error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize pe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize fe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Framing Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize bi & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break interrupt bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize thre & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter holding register empty bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize temt & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter empty bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receiver fifo error bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.7 MSR}{anchor220}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.7 MSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.7 MSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dcts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta clear to send. This is used to indicate that the modem control line cts\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ddsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data set ready. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dsr\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize teri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Trailing edge of ring indicator. This is used to indicate that a change on the input ri\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[2](Out1) has changed state from a high to low. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ddcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data carrier detect. This is used to indicate that a change on line dcd\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[3](Out1) has changed. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize cts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Clear to send. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line cts\_n. \newline
0: cts\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: cts\_n input is asserted(logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data set ready. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dsr\_n. \newline
0: dsr\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dsr\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize ri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Ring indicator. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line ri\_n. \newline
0: ri\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: ri\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[2](Out1). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dcd\_n. \newline
0: dcd\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dcd\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[3](Out2). \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.8 SCR}{anchor221}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.8 SCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.8 SCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003301C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize scr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is for programmers to use as a temporary storage space. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.9 LPDLL}{anchor222}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.9 LPDLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.9 LPDLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the lower 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.10 LPDLH}{anchor223}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.10 LPDLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.10 LPDLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdlh & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the upper 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.11 FAR}{anchor224}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.11 FAR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.11 FAR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033070}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize fa & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  FIFO access regiseter. 1: FIFO access mode is enabled. 0: disabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.12 TFR}{anchor225}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.12 TFR}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.12 TFR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033074}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO read. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.13 RFW}{anchor226}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.13 RFW}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.13 RFW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033078}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfwd & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo write data. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize rfpe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo parity error. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize rffe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo framing error. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.14 USR}{anchor227}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.14 USR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.14 USR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003307C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: uart is idle or inactive; 1: uart is busy. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tfnf & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO not full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize rfne & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO not empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize rff & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.15 TFL}{anchor228}

\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfl & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This indicates the number of data entries in the transmit fifo. \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{20.16 RFL}{anchor229}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.16 RFL}

\pdfbookmark[2]{20.17 HTX}{anchor230}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.17 HTX}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.17 HTX} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x400330A4}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize htx & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to halt transmissions for testing, so that the transmit fifo can be filled by the master. 0: halt tx disabled; 1: halt tx enabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{20.18 DMASA}{anchor231}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{20.18 DMASA}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{20.18 DMASA} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x400330A8}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dmasa & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to perform a DMA software acknowledge if a transfer needs to be terminated due to an error condition. For example, if the DMA disables the channel, then the DW\_apb\_uart should clear its request. This cause the tx request, tx single, rx request and rx signle signals to de-assert.  This bit is 'self-clearing' \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{uart2}
\section{uart2}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}{anchor234}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rbr\_thr\_dll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  receiver buffer when read, transmit holding register when write(In fifo mode, the written data will push to tx fifo).\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (low) bit [7:0]. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.2 IER\_DLH}{anchor235}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.2 IER\_DLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.2 IER\_DLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize erbfi\_dlh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx data available interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize etbei\_dlh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Transmit Holding Register Empty interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize elsi\_dlh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx Line Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 2. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize edssi\_dlh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Modem Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 3. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize dlh\_6\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  reserved;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit [6:4]. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_dlh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Programmable THRE interrupt mode enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 7. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.3 IIR\_FCR}{anchor236}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.3 IIR\_FCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.3 IIR\_FCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize iid\_0\_fifoe & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 0; Write:  fifo enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize iid\_1\_rfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 1; Write:  rx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize iid\_2\_xfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 2; Write:  tx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize iid\_3\_dmam & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 3; Write:  dma mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize tet & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx Empty Trigger. 0: fifo empty. 1: 2 characters in the fifo. 2: fifo 1/4 full. 3: fifo 1/2 full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize fifose\_rcvr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx trigger. 0: 1 character in the fifo. 1: fifo 1/4 full. 2: fifo 1/2 full. 3: fifo 2 less than full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.4 LCR}{anchor237}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.4 LCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.4 LCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003400C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize dls & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data length select. 0: 5bit, 1: 6bit, 2: 7bit, 3:8bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize stop & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of stop bits. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize pen & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize eps & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Even Parity select. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize stick\_parity & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Stick parity. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize bc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break control bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dlab & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Divisor latch access bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.5 MCR}{anchor238}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.5 MCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.5 MCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dtr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data terminal ready. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize rts & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request to send. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize out1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT1. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output1 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize out2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT2.  This is used to directly control the user-designated Output2 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize lb & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Loopback mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize afce & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Auto flow control enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize sire & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SIR mode enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.6 LSR}{anchor239}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.6 LSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.6 LSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data ready bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize oe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Overrun error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize pe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize fe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Framing Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize bi & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break interrupt bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize thre & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter holding register empty bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize temt & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter empty bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receiver fifo error bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.7 MSR}{anchor240}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.7 MSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.7 MSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dcts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta clear to send. This is used to indicate that the modem control line cts\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ddsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data set ready. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dsr\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize teri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Trailing edge of ring indicator. This is used to indicate that a change on the input ri\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[2](Out1) has changed state from a high to low. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ddcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data carrier detect. This is used to indicate that a change on line dcd\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[3](Out1) has changed. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize cts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Clear to send. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line cts\_n. \newline
0: cts\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: cts\_n input is asserted(logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data set ready. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dsr\_n. \newline
0: dsr\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dsr\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize ri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Ring indicator. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line ri\_n. \newline
0: ri\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: ri\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[2](Out1). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dcd\_n. \newline
0: dcd\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dcd\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[3](Out2). \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.8 SCR}{anchor241}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.8 SCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.8 SCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003401C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize scr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is for programmers to use as a temporary storage space. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.9 LPDLL}{anchor242}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.9 LPDLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.9 LPDLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the lower 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.10 LPDLH}{anchor243}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.10 LPDLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.10 LPDLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdlh & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the upper 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.11 FAR}{anchor244}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.11 FAR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.11 FAR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034070}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize fa & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  FIFO access regiseter. 1: FIFO access mode is enabled. 0: disabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.12 TFR}{anchor245}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.12 TFR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.12 TFR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034074}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO read. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.13 RFW}{anchor246}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.13 RFW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.13 RFW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034078}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfwd & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo write data. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize rfpe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo parity error. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize rffe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo framing error. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.14 USR}{anchor247}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.14 USR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.14 USR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003407C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: uart is idle or inactive; 1: uart is busy. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tfnf & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO not full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize rfne & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO not empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize rff & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.15 TFL}{anchor248}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.15 TFL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.15 TFL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034080}\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfl & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This indicates the number of data entries in the transmit fifo. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.16 RFL}{anchor249}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.16 RFL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.16 RFL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034084}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfl & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This indicates the number of data entries in the receive fifo. \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.17 HTX}{anchor250}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.17 HTX}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.17 HTX} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x400340A4}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize htx & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to halt transmissions for testing, so that the transmit fifo can be filled by the master. 0: halt tx disabled; 1: halt tx enabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.18 DMASA}{anchor251}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.18 DMASA}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.18 DMASA} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x400340A8}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dmasa & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to perform a DMA software acknowledge if a transfer needs to be terminated due to an error condition. For example, if the DMA disables the channel, then the DW\_apb\_uart should clear its request. This cause the tx request, tx single, rx request and rx signle signals to de-assert.  This bit is 'self-clearing' \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{23.1 ctrl}{anchor318}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{23.1 ctrl}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{23.1 ctrl} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40036000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 : 0 & \footnotesize endian & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Endian Type\newline
SD TX FIFO Endian\newline
3'b000: \{pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[0]\}\newline
3'b001: \{pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[3]\}\newline
3'b010: \{pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[2]\}\newline
3'b011: pwdata\_bit[0-31]\newline
3'b100: \{pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[1]\}\newline
default: 32'h0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize reset\_n & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize Software reset, active Low\newline
Software reset for TX and RX FIFOs of Sd host, active low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize dma\_rx\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable rx dma req triggered when rxfifo level arrived dma\_rx\_th \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dma\_tx\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable tx dma req triggered when txfifo level arrived dma\_tx\_th \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize dma\_rx\_th & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Rx dma req triggered threshold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 : 16 & \footnotesize dma\_tx\_th & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Tx dma req triggered threshold \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize aud\_proc\_enab\-le & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable aud\_proc \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize aud\_proc\_adc\_\-path\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  reset aud\_proc adc path \\
