1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize lsb\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  When high, the output data format is with the least significant bit first.\newline
0:: MSB\newline
1:: LSB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize right\_justifie\-d\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  right justified mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 5 & \footnotesize bck\_lrck\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure extend cycles of bck after data transfer is finished.\newline
Voice\_Mode:: each sample takes wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck BCLK cycle. \newline
Audio\_Mode:: each sample takes 2*(wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck) BCLK cycle. 2 times than Voice \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 10 & \footnotesize wlen\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  i2s data length\newline
00: 16-bit\newline
01: 20-bit\newline
10: 24-bit\newline
11: 32-bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize lrck\_pol\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure LRCK polarity. \newline
0:: LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L:    high level on LRCK means left channel, low level on LRCK means right channel. \newline
1:: LEFT\_L\_RIGHT\_H:    high level on LRCK means right channel, low level on LRCK means left channel. \newline
Note: this bit should be set to '0' (LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L) in voice mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize bck\_pol\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  if Master Mode, invert BCLK out. if slave Mode, invert BCLK in.\newline
0:: NORMAL\newline
1:: INVERT \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize bckout\_gate\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sets the BckOut gating. This bit decide if aud intf continue to output BCK clock after data has been sent.\newline
0:: NO\_GATE\newline
1:: GATED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize rx\_half\_cycle\-\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio input data by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize tx\_lrck\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio LRCK by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize rx\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Indicates the delay between serial data in MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
2'b00:: ALIGN:    Digital audio in MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b01:: DLY\_1:   Digital audio in MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b10:: DLY\_2:   Digital audio in MSB is 2 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b11:: DLY\_3:   Digital audio in MSB is 3 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_s\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  ONLY for slave mode:: configure 1 cycle supplementary Tx delay. \newline
0:: NO\_DLY:  No supplementary Tx delay. \newline
1:: DLY:         One Cycle supplementary Tx delay. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure the delay between serial data out MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
0:: ALIGN:           Digital audio out MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
1:: DLY\_1:          Digital audio out MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize master\_mode\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  configure aud intf works in master mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated internally) or slave mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated externally).\newline
0:: SLAVE\newline
1:: MASTER \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 : 22 & \footnotesize rx\_serial\_mod\-e\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 : 25 & \footnotesize tx\_serial\_mod\-e\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize tx\_off\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Disable aud intf Tx functions. Important:: if you want to do record only, you must set this bit otherwise aud intf state machine will not start. \newline
0:: TX\_ON:    Both Tx Rx enabled. \newline
1:: TX\_OFF:   Rx enabled only, Tx disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 29 & \footnotesize tx\_mode\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure mono or stereo format for Audio data out:\newline
2'b00:: STEREO\_STEREO:  stereo input from DMA, stereo output to pin. \newline
2'b01:: MONOL\_STEREO\_DUPLI:   mono channel L input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b10:: MONOR\_STEREO\_DUPLI:  mono channel R input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b11:: MONO\_IN\_MONO\_OUT\newline
Funtion mono input from DMA, stereo output in left channel to pin, can be implemented by setting aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[1] or aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[0] disable.\newline
When works in DAI, or DSD mode, always select "00" mode STEREO\_STEREO. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize i2s\_enable\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable i2s interface \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{24.36 aud\_intf\_i2s\_cfg1}

\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize swap\_chlr\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Swap Channel L and Channel R.[0]\newline
[0] for output channel swap, 0: left, 1:right\newline
[1] for input channel swap, 0: left, 1: right \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tx\_data\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio data out by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize lsb\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  When high, the output data format is with the least significant bit first.\newline
0:: MSB\newline
1:: LSB \\

Voice\_Mode:: each sample takes wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck BCLK cycle. \newline
Audio\_Mode:: each sample takes 2*(wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck) BCLK cycle. 2 times than Voice \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 10 & \footnotesize wlen\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  i2s data length\newline
00: 16-bit\newline
01: 20-bit\newline
10: 24-bit\newline
11: 32-bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize lrck\_pol\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure LRCK polarity. \newline
0:: LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L:    high level on LRCK means left channel, low level on LRCK means right channel. \newline
1:: LEFT\_L\_RIGHT\_H:    high level on LRCK means right channel, low level on LRCK means left channel. \newline
Note: this bit should be set to '0' (LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L) in voice mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize bck\_pol\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  if Master Mode, invert BCLK out. if slave Mode, invert BCLK in.\newline
0:: NORMAL\newline
1:: INVERT \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize bckout\_gate\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sets the BckOut gating. This bit decide if aud intf continue to output BCK clock after data has been sent.\newline
0:: NO\_GATE\newline
1:: GATED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize rx\_half\_cycle\-\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio input data by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize tx\_lrck\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio output LRCK by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize rx\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Indicates the delay between serial data in MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
2'b00:: ALIGN:    Digital audio in MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b01:: DLY\_1:   Digital audio in MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b10:: DLY\_2:   Digital audio in MSB is 2 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b11:: DLY\_3:   Digital audio in MSB is 3 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_s\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  ONLY for slave mode:: configure 1 cycle supplementary Tx delay. \newline
0:: NO\_DLY:  No supplementary Tx delay. \newline
1:: DLY:         One Cycle supplementary Tx delay. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure the delay between serial data out MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
0:: ALIGN:           Digital audio out MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
1:: DLY\_1:          Digital audio out MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize master\_mode\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  configure aud intf works in master mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated internally) or slave mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated externally).\newline
0:: SLAVE\newline
1:: MASTER \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 : 22 & \footnotesize rx\_serial\_mod\-e\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 : 25 & \footnotesize tx\_serial\_mod\-e\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize tx\_off\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Disable aud intf Tx functions. Important:: if you want to do record only, you must set this bit otherwise aud intf state machine will not start. \newline
0:: TX\_ON:    Both Tx Rx enabled. \newline
1:: TX\_OFF:   Rx enabled only, Tx disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 29 & \footnotesize tx\_mode\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure mono or stereo format for Audio data out:\newline
2'b00:: STEREO\_STEREO:             stereo input from DMA, stereo output to pin. \newline
2'b01:: MONOL\_STEREO\_DUPLI:   mono channel L input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b10:: MONOR\_STEREO\_DUPLI:  mono channel R input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline

When works in DAI, or DSD mode, always select "00" mode STEREO\_STEREO. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize i2s\_enable\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable i2s interface \\
\hline

\hline

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize read\_state\_bu\-sy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Read state busy. \\

\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{25.6 flash\_config}{anchor429}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{25.6 flash\_config}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{25.6 flash\_config} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40038014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize quad\_mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Quad mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wproctect\_pin & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Set wproctect pin active. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize hold\_pin & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Set hold pin active. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize dual\_mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Dual mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize sample\_delay & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x2 & \footnotesize Sample delay. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize din\_cap\_edge & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize sample clock edge selector,\newline
0: posedge of clk spiflash\newline
1: negedge of clk spiflash \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 8 & \footnotesize clk\_divider & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x8 & \footnotesize Clock divider. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize cmd\_quad & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Quad Command Enable. Hardware will regard current command as quad command if this bit is active. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{25.10 nand\_cfg1}{anchor433}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{25.10 nand\_cfg1}

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{25.10 nand\_cfg1} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40038024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize nand\_sel & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Internal nand flash selection. Nand flash also can be selected from pin. 1'b0: Nor FLASH; 1'b1: Nand flash. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 : 1 & \footnotesize nand\_addr\_26\_\-25 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Bit 26 to 25 of nand\_address. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 8 & \footnotesize page\_read\_cmd & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x13 & \footnotesize Page read command. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 23 : 16 & \footnotesize get\_sts\_cmd & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xF & \footnotesize Get status command. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 24 & \footnotesize ram\_read\_cmd & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize RAM read command. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{wdg0}
\section{wdg0}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor568}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor569}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor570}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor571}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010300C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor572}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor573}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor574}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103C00}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{spi}
\section{spi}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.1 SPI\_TXD}{anchor582}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.1 SPI\_TXD}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.1 SPI\_TXD} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Write data to tx fifo
Read data from rx fifo}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_TXD\_RXD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write data to this address initiates a  character transmission through TX FIFO\newline
Read from this address retrieve data from  RX FIFO \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.2 SPI\_CLKD}{anchor583}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.2 SPI\_CLKD}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.2 SPI\_CLKD} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Clock divider bit 15 to 0.divider is 2(n+1)}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_CLKD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize Specify the clock ratio between spi\_sck and clk\_spi\newline
spi\_sck = clk\_spi/2(SPI\_CLKD + 1) \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.3 SPI\_CTL0}{anchor584}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.3 SPI\_CTL0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.3 SPI\_CTL0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105008}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize NG\_RX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable RX data shift in at clock neg-edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize NG\_TX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  "1" enable TX data shift out at clock neg-edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 2 & \footnotesize CHNL\_LEN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" 32 bits per word\newline
"1" 1 bits per word \newline
"31" 31 bits per word \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize LSB & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1": enable transmit data from LSB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize SPI\_CSN\_PRE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xF & \footnotesize  "1": cs is invalid \newline
"0": cs is valid \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize Reserved & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Reserved \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SCK\_REV & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SCK inverse enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize SYNC\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" : sync mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize SYNC\_POL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sync polarity, positive or negative pulse for SPI or 3-wire mode, read command polarity \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.4 SPI\_CTL1}{anchor585}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.4 SPI\_CTL1}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.4 SPI\_CTL1} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010500C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize S3W\_POS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  3-wire mode, w/r control position or the sync pulse position \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize S3W\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable 3-wire mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize CS\_H\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  3-wire melody timing 1, csn high mode enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize S8\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable S8 mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize SYN\_CSN\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  S8 CD or SYNC signal maps to csn number\newline
0x1 selects csn0 as cd signal\newline
0x2 selects csn1 as cd signal\newline
in SPI\_HS it must be 0x0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize RTX\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0x00":idle mode\newline
"0x01":receive mode\newline
"0x10":transmit mode\newline
"0x11":transmit and receive \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.5 SPI\_CTL2}{anchor586}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.5 SPI\_CTL2}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.5 SPI\_CTL2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105010}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize S3W\_RD\_START & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read date start bit, used for 3-wire mode and 3-wire 9bit RW mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize IS\_SLVD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" slave,only support microplus mode\newline
"0" master \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize DMA\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" : enable DMA \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize RX\_ONLY\_HLD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : working on only receive mode, when rxf\_realfull is high, SPI will be held until rxf realfull is low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize RX\_DMA\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : rx\_dma\_req keep high until receiving the rx\_dma\_ack\newline
"1" : rx\_dma\_req is high when rx full is high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize TX\_DMA\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : tx\_dma\_req keep high until receiving the tx\_dma\_ack\newline
"1" : tx\_dma\_req is high when tx full is high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize DMA\_REQ\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable 3-wire mode \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.6 SPI\_CTL3}{anchor587}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.6 SPI\_CTL3}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.6 SPI\_CTL3} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105014}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_THLD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x10 & \footnotesize  Receive data empty threshold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY\_THL\-D & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x10 & \footnotesize  Receive data full threshold \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.7 SPI\_CTL4}{anchor588}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.7 SPI\_CTL4}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.7 SPI\_CTL4} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105018}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 0 & \footnotesize BLOCK\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of data words ready to receive in "receive only" mode.\newline
Only used for master mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize IS\_RX\_ONLY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1":receive data only.The bits should be written at last.\newline
Only used for master mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SYNC\_HALF & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sync half,sync width is half spi\_sck cycle \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SYNC\_CLKMASK & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" Mask out the first clock pulse in SPI mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize PHS\_DLY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Phase delay. Relate to fast mode\newline
When in normal mode,this bit is not used. Only used for slave mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize IS\_FST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0": normal mode\newline
"1": fast mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize RX\_ONLY\_DO & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0": SPI send all 0 to slave\newline
"1": SPI send all 1 to slave \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.8 SPI\_CTL5}{anchor589}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.8 SPI\_CTL5}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.8 SPI\_CTL5} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010501C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize ITVL\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize For master, transmit data interval, programmabl n from 0 to 65535, delay is (4*n+3) clock cycle\newline
For slave, max receive data interval. If slave has not sampled data,in the interval (4*n+3) spi\_clk,slave will stop the receive process and send timeout interrupt \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.9 SPI\_INT\_EN}{anchor590}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.9 SPI\_INT\_EN}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.9 SPI\_INT\_EN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105020}\\

\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI interrupt enable register}\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FUL\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx\_fifo\_full interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RX\_OVF\_INT\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx\_overrun\_reg interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize TIME\_OUT\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Slave mode timeout interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx\_fifo\_full interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_INT\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx\_fifo\_empty interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TXF\_END\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx end interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RXF\_END\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx end interrupt enable \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.10 SPI\_INT\_CLR}{anchor591}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.10 SPI\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.10 SPI\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105024}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI interrupt clear register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Rx\_fifo\_full interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY\_INT\-\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Rx\_fifo\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FULL\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Tx\_fifo\_full interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_INT\-\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Tx\_fifo\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RX\_OVF\_INT\_C\-LR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Rx\_overrun\_reg interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize TIME\_OUT\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear slave mode timeout interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TXF\_END\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx data end interrupt clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RXF\_END\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx data end interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS}{anchor592}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105028}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI interrupt raw status}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize RX\_FIFO\_FULL\_\-R & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx\_fifo\_full\_r \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY\_RAW\-\_STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw rx\_fifo\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FULL\_RAW\_\-STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw Tx\_fifo\_full interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TX\_FIFO\_EMPTY\-\_W & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw txf\_fifo\_empty\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RX\_OVF\_RAW\_S\-TS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw Rx\_overrun\_reg interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize TIME\_OUT\_RAW\_\-STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw slave mode time out interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_RAW\_\-STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw rxf\_full interrupt. when the number of rx fifo data byte is larger than the the rx empty watermark value.Atuo clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_RAW\-\_STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw txf\_empty interrupt. when the number of tx fifo data byte is less than the the tx empty watermark value.Atuo clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TX\_END\_IRQ & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw tx data end interrupt. bit is set when spi controller send TX\_DATA\_LEN data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RX\_END\_IRQ & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw rx data end interrupt. bit is set when spi controller receive RX\_DATA\_LEN data \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.12 SPI\_INT\_MASK\_STS}

\pdfbookmark[2]{31.13 SPI\_STS1}{anchor594}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.13 SPI\_STS1}

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.13 SPI\_STS1} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105030}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI status register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_RD\_ADDR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO read address \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize RXF\_WR\_ADDR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO write address \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.14 SPI\_STS2}{anchor595}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.14 SPI\_STS2}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.14 SPI\_STS2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105034}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI status register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of RX FIFO data byte is larger than the RX full watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of RX FIFO data byte is less than the RX empty interrupt watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of TX FIFO data byte is larger than the TX full watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of TX FIFO  data byte is less than the TX empty watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RXF\_REALFULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO is real full.(not relates to register RXF\_FULL\_THLD) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize RXF\_REALEMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO is real empty. RX FIFO has no data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize TXF\_REALFULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  TX FIFO is real full.(not relates to register TXF\_FULL\_THLD) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_REALEMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  TX FIFO is real empty. TX FIFO has no data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize BUSY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1": busy, transmit process\newline
"0": idle state \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SPI\_RXD & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_rxd(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SPI\_TXD & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_txd(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SPI\_SCK & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_sck(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SPI\_CS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_cs(monitor) \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.15 DSP\_WAIT}{anchor596}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.15 DSP\_WAIT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.15 DSP\_WAIT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105038}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Use for DSP control}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_DSPWAIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This register is used for DSP control \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize RX\_DATA\_SWT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read data switch:\newline
0: rdata[31:0] = pdata[31:0]\newline
1: rdata = \{pdata[7:0], pdata[15:8], pdata[23:16],  pdata[31:24]\}\newline
2: rdata = \{pdata[15:0],pdata[31:16]\} \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize TX\_DATA\_SWT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write data switch:\newline
0: wdata[31:0] = pdata[31:0]\newline
1: wdata = \{pdata[7:0], pdata[15:8], pdata[23:16],  pdata[31:24]\}\newline
2: wdata = \{pdata[15:0],pdata[31:16]\} \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{31.18 SPI\_STS4}{anchor599}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.18 SPI\_STS4}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.18 SPI\_STS4} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105044}\\

\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize TXF\_WR\_ADDR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  TX FIFO write address \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.19 SPI\_FIFO\_RST}{anchor600}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.19 SPI\_FIFO\_RST}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.19 SPI\_FIFO\_RST} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105048}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI tx/rx fifo reset bit}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_FIFO\_RST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1":reset all FIFOs \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{31.20 SPI\_CTL7}{anchor601}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{31.20 SPI\_CTL7}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{31.20 SPI\_CTL7} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010504C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CSN\_IE\_CTL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CSN IE output set\newline
0: not support csn input\newline
1: support csn input \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 : 1 & \footnotesize CSN\_I\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CSN select control:\newline
0: CSN 0    1: CSN 1    2: CSN 2    3: CSN 3 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 3 & \footnotesize SPI\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Used for master only\newline
0: SPI\_MODE disable\newline
1: 3 wire 9 bit, cd bit, SDI/SDO share one IO\newline
2: 3 wire 9 bit, cd bit, SDI,SDO\newline
3: 4 wire 8 bit, cd pin, SDI/SDO\newline
4: 4 wire 8 bit, cd pin, SDI,SDO \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize TX\_CMD\_SET & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: select fmark as the dma request\newline
0:select software dma request \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize SPI\_TX\_HLD\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: enable ahb2apb bridge write hold when tx fifo full\newline
0: disable ahb2apb bridge write hold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SPI\_RX\_HLD\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: enable ahb2apb bridge read hold when rx fifo empty\newline
0: disable ahb2apb bridge write hold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_CLK\_\-SEl & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Used when SPI slave in High speed mode\newline
1:enable spi slave rtx\newline
0:disable spi slave rtx \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: SPI slave in low speed mode\newline
0: SPI slave in low speed mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize RGB\_888\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize RGB\_666\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize RGB\_565\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize DATA\_LINE2\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{timer0}
\section{timer0}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.1 TIMER0\_CTL}{anchor612}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.1 TIMER0\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.1 TIMER0\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 reset, active high, cleared automatically\newline
When one, the Timer Counter and the Prescale Counter are synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer0\_mrr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Reset on MR: the TC will be reset if MR matches it.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_mrs & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Stop on MR: the TC and PC will be stopped and timer0\_en will be set to 0 if MR matches the TC.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist\newline
0, tick divider exists \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.2 TIMER0\_TC}{anchor613}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.2 TIMER0\_TC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.2 TIMER0\_TC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 counter\newline
The 32-bit TC is incremented every PR+1 number of TICKs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.3 TIMER0\_PR}{anchor614}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.3 TIMER0\_PR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.3 TIMER0\_PR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_pr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer0 prescale register \newline
When the Prescale Counter (below) is equal to this value(total timer0\_pr + 1 cycles), the next clock increments the TC and clears the PC. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.4 TIMER0\_PC}{anchor615}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.4 TIMER0\_PC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.4 TIMER0\_PC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010600C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_pc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 prescale counter \newline
The 32-bit PC is a counter which is incremented\newline
to the value stored in PR. When the value in PR is reached, the TC\newline
is incremented and the PC is cleared. The PC is observable and\newline
controllable through the bus interface. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.5 TIMER0\_MR}{anchor616}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.5 TIMER0\_MR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.5 TIMER0\_MR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_mr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer0 match register \newline
MR can be enabled through the MRR to reset\newline
the TC, the MRS to stop both the TC and PC, and/or generate an interrupt\newline
every time MR matches the TC \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}{anchor617}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value\newline
4'd0: disable tick\newline
4'dN(N great than 0): tick is div(N+1) from rtc clock \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_up & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value update, cleared automatically \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}{anchor618}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 wrap interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 wrap interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 wrap interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.8 TIMER0\_INT\_STATUS}{anchor619}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.8 TIMER0\_INT\_STATUS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.8 TIMER0\_INT\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010601C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_raw\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match interrupt raw status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_masked\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match interrupt masked status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_raw\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 wrap interrupt raw status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_masked\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 wrap interrupt masked status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.9 TIMER1\_CTL}{anchor620}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.9 TIMER1\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.9 TIMER1\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer1\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 reset, active high, cleared automatically\newline
When one, the Timer Counter and the Prescale Counter are synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer1\_mrr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Reset on MR: the TC will be reset if MR matches it.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer1\_mrs & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Stop on MR: the TC and PC will be stopped and timer1\_en will be set to 0 if MR matches the TC.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer1\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER1\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist\newline
0, tick divider exists \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.10 TIMER1\_TC}{anchor621}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.10 TIMER1\_TC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.10 TIMER1\_TC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_tc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 counter\newline
The 32-bit TC is incremented every PR+1 number of TICKs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.11 TIMER1\_PR}{anchor622}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.11 TIMER1\_PR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.11 TIMER1\_PR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106028}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_pr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer1 prescale register \newline
When the Prescale Counter (below) is equal to this value(total timer1\_pr + 1 cycles), the next clock increments the TC and clears the PC. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.12 TIMER1\_PC}{anchor623}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.12 TIMER1\_PC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.12 TIMER1\_PC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010602C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_pc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 prescale counter \newline
The 32-bit PC is a counter which is incremented\newline
to the value stored in PR. When the value in PR is reached, the TC\newline
is incremented and the PC is cleared. The PC is observable and\newline
controllable through the bus interface. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.13 TIMER1\_MR}{anchor624}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.13 TIMER1\_MR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.13 TIMER1\_MR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106030}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_mr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer1 match register \newline
MR can be enabled through the MRR to reset\newline
the TC, the MRS to stop both the TC and PC, and/or generate an interrupt\newline
every time MR matches the TC \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.14 TIMER1\_TICK\_DIV}{anchor625}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.14 TIMER1\_TICK\_DIV}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.14 TIMER1\_TICK\_DIV} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106034}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_tick\_d\-iv & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer1 tick div value\newline
4'd0: disable tick\newline
4'dN(N great than 0): tick is div(N+1) from rtc clock \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer1\_tick\_d\-iv\_up & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 tick div value update, cleared automatically \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{32.15 TIMER1\_INT\_CTRL}{anchor626}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{32.15 TIMER1\_INT\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{32.15 TIMER1\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40106038}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_match\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer1 match interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer1 match interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer1\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer1 wrap interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer1 wrap interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize timer1\_match\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer1 match interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer1\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer1 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer1\_match\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 match interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize timer1\_wrap\_i\-nt\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 wrap interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match interrupt clear, active high \\

\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{33.9 TIMER1\_CTL}{anchor644}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{33.9 TIMER1\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{33.9 TIMER1\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40107020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer1\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 reset, active high, cleared automatically\newline
When one, the Timer Counter and the Prescale Counter are synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer1\_mrr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Reset on MR: the TC will be reset if MR matches it.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer1\_mrs & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Stop on MR: the TC and PC will be stopped and timer1\_en will be set to 0 if MR matches the TC.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer1\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER1\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist\newline
0, tick divider exists \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{33.10 TIMER1\_TC}{anchor645}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{33.10 TIMER1\_TC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{33.10 TIMER1\_TC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40107024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_tc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 counter\newline
The 32-bit TC is incremented every PR+1 number of TICKs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{33.11 TIMER1\_PR}{anchor646}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{33.11 TIMER1\_PR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{33.11 TIMER1\_PR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40107028}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_pr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer1 prescale register \newline
When the Prescale Counter (below) is equal to this value(total timer1\_pr + 1 cycles), the next clock increments the TC and clears the PC. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{33.12 TIMER1\_PC}{anchor647}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{33.12 TIMER1\_PC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{33.12 TIMER1\_PC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010702C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_pc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer1 prescale counter \newline
The 32-bit PC is a counter which is incremented\newline
to the value stored in PR. When the value in PR is reached, the TC\newline
is incremented and the PC is cleared. The PC is observable and\newline
controllable through the bus interface. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{33.13 TIMER1\_MR}{anchor648}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{33.13 TIMER1\_MR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{33.13 TIMER1\_MR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40107030}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_mr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer1 match register \newline
MR can be enabled through the MRR to reset\newline
the TC, the MRS to stop both the TC and PC, and/or generate an interrupt\newline
every time MR matches the TC \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer1\_match\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer1 match interrupt enable set\newline

0: Ignored.\newline

1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\

0: Ignored.\newline

0: not Enabled. \\

0: Ignored. \\

\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer1\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer1 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\

\footnotesize 23 : 16 & \footnotesize sLfsrGen0Q & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  LFSR gen0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 24 & \footnotesize sLfsrGen1Q & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  LFSR gen1 \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize GPIOIS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFF & \footnotesize  "1" detect signals level\newline
"0" detect signals edge \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{37.5 GPIOIBE}{anchor676}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{37.5 GPIOIBE}

\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize GPIOIBE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" both edges trigger an interrupt,\newline
"0" interrupt generation event is controlled by  GPIOIEV \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{37.6 GPIOIEV}{anchor677}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{37.6 GPIOIEV}

\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize GPIOIEV & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFF & \footnotesize  GPIO bits interrupt event register:\newline
"1" high levels trigger interrupts,\newline
"0" low levels trigger interrupts. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{37.7 GPIOIE}{anchor678}

\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize GPIOIE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  GPIO bits interrupt enable register:\newline
"1" corresponding bit interrupt is enabled.\newline
"0" corresponding bit interrupt isn't enabled \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{37.8 GPIORIS}{anchor679}

"1" interrupt condition met\newline
"0" condition not met \\

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.1 EFUSE\_ALL0\_INDEX} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C008}\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_ALL0\_ST\-ART\_INDEX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize All 0 check start index \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.2 EFUSE\_MODE\_CTRL}{anchor687}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.2 EFUSE\_MODE\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.2 EFUSE\_MODE\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C00C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_ALL0\_CH\-ECK\_START & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 to this bit will trigger all0 check to efuse, this bit is self-clear\newline
,read this bit will always get 0.(use PREADY) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 1 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg}{anchor688}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_IP\_VER & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x100 & \footnotesize IP version, now is r1p0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_TYPE & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Efuse type: 00 TSMC \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 18 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.4 EFUSE\_CFG0}{anchor689}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.4 EFUSE\_CFG0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.4 EFUSE\_CFG0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 0 & \footnotesize TPGM\_TIME\_CNT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x140 & \footnotesize Program strobe high time. If set n, the Tpgm time will last for (n+1) clk\_efuse cycle, only when PGM\_EN=1 can write this field. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 9 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 23 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_STROBE\_\-LOW\_WIDTH & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1C & \footnotesize This counter is used to control STROBE signal low level width in PGM mode, for TSMC efuse memory, no extra requirement for this signal, For 26Mhz efuse controller clock, by default, this width will be: 38.4*28=1075ns > 1us.\newline
If you want to speed up program speed, can configure the register to a smaller value. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 24 & \footnotesize CLK\_EFS\_DIV & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Clk\_efs divider, if this value is n, the frequency of controller will be divided by (n+1) from clk\_efs.\newline
In most case, this field not need to change. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN}{anchor690}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C040}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse vdd enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize SEC\_AUTO\_CHEC\-K\_ENABLE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize program read back auto-check enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize DOUBLE\_BIT\_EN\-\_SEC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse double bit enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize SEC\_MARGIN\_RD\-\_ENABLE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse margin read mode enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_LOCK\_BIT\_\-WR\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize If set the bit, lock bits will be written after PGM process. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 5 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG}{anchor691}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C044}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize block\_auto\_ch\-eck\_failed & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates block auto check failed after programming. If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize shadow\_block\_\-auto\_check\_failed & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates shadow block  auto check failed after programming if double\_bit\_en is set.\newline
If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_2 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_PRO\-T\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates block auto check failed after programming. If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_PRO\-T\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates shadow block is protected and can not be programmed if double\_bit\_en is set.\newline
If SW send a PGM command to memory block[i], and the controller found this memory block is protected(which means the highest bit is 1), this bit will set to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize reserved\_1 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates write process without setting pg\_en to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_ON\_R\-D\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates read process without setting vdd\_on to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates arbiter read block0 which may indicates unexpected access. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates write process without setting magic number. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_\-FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit enk1 and enk2 is not switch correctly. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SEC\_ALL0\_CHEC\-K\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates all 0 check fail. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 14 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR}{anchor692}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C048}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_ERR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD0\_ERR\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_ERR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD1\_ERR\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_2 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_PRO\-T\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD0\_PROT\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_PRO\-T\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD1\_PROT\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize reserved\_1 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_ON\_R\-D\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_VDD\_ON\_RD\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SEC\_ALL0\_CHEC\-K\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_ALL0\_CHECK\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 14 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER}{anchor693}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C04C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_EFUSE\_MAG\-IC\_NUMBER & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Magic number, only when this field is 0x8800, the efuse programming command can be handle.\newline
Set the magic number right will lock the power switch and PGM enable.\newline
So if SW want to program efuse memory, except open clocks and power, 2 other conditions must be met :\newline
(1) SEC\_EFUSE\_MAGIC\_NUMBER =0x8800\newline
(2) PG\_EN=1;\newline
(3) Switch the power right; \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 16 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG}{anchor694}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C050}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CONF\_PROT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SEC/NON-SEC address configure. \newline
1: indicates SEC can access EFUSE\_CFG0;\newline
0: indicates NON-SEC can access EFUSE\_CFG0; \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ACCESS\_PROT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize 0: SEC access; 1: NON SEC address enable switch \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{38.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT}{anchor695}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
