\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C054}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize EFS\_ENK1\_ON & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VDDQ power switch K2, to safely control this power switch. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize EFS\_ENK2\_ON & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize VDDQ power switch K2, to safely control this power switch. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize NS\_S\_PG\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize set this bit will open static power supply for efuse memory, before any operation towards to efuse memory this bit have to set to 1. once this bit is cleared, the efuse will go to power down mose. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_READ\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize read mode, 1'b1: sar mode; 1'b0: normal mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_LCH\_BIAS\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR bias mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 5 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_DLY\_BIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR delay bits \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 9 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_BIAS\_BIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR bias bit control \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 : 12 & \footnotesize SAR\_READ\_BITS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Read bits index for SAR mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 17 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{40.13 GPVNDCTL}{anchor775}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{40.13 GPVNDCTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{40.13 GPVNDCTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40200034}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize RegData & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Data (RegData)\newline
Contains the write data for register write.\newline
Read data for register read, valid when VStatus Done is set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 8 & \footnotesize VCtrl & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize UTMI+ Vendor Control Register Address (VCtrl) The 4-bit register\newline
address a vendor defined 4-bit parallel output bus. Bits 11:8 of this\newline
field are placed on utmi\_vcontrol[3:0]. ULPI Extended Register\newline
Address (ExtRegAddr) The 6-bit PHY extended register address. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 : 16 & \footnotesize RegAddr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Address (RegAddr)\newline
The 6-bit PHY register address for immediate PHY Register set\newline
access. Set to 6'h2F for Extended PHY Register set access. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 22 & \footnotesize RegWr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Write (RegWr) Set this bit for register writes, and clear it\newline
for register reads. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 25 & \footnotesize NewRegReq & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize New Register Request (NewRegReq)\newline
The application sets this bit for a new vendor control access.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (INACTIVE): New Register Request not active\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE): New Register Request active \\
\hline
\footnotesize 26 & \footnotesize VStsBsy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VStatus Busy (VStsBsy)\newline
The core sets this bit when the vendor control access is in progress\newline
and clears this bit when done. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 & \footnotesize VStsDone & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VStatus Done (VStsDone)\newline
The core sets this bit when the vendor control access is done. This\newline
bit is cleared by the core when the application sets the New\newline
Register Request bit (bit 25).\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (INACTIVE): VStatus Done inactive\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE): VStatus Done active \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{timer2}
\section{timer2}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{45.1 TIMER0\_CTL}{anchor1296}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{45.1 TIMER0\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{45.1 TIMER0\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 reset, active high, cleared automatically.\newline
When one, the Timer Counter is synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist.\newline
0, tick divider exists. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tc\_hi\_\-dis & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, the High 16bit of TC and MR is ignored when generating match/wrap interrupt.\newline
0, this feature is ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 8 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_f\-req & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick frequency, used to select TC and adjust counter increment value.\newline
2'b00: tick is 64KHz\newline
2'b01: tick is 64KHz\newline
2'b10: tick is 32KHz\newline
2'b11: tick is 16KHz \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match0 enable.\newline
0, match0 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match1 enable.\newline
0, match1 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize timer0\_match2\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match2 enable.\newline
0, match2 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize timer0\_match3\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match3 enable.\newline
0, match3 disable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{45.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}{anchor1297}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{45.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{45.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value\newline
2'b00: disable tick\newline
2'b01: tick is div2 from rtc clock\newline
2'b10: tick is div4 from rtc clock\newline
2'b11: tick is div8 from rtc clock \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_up & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value update, cleared automatically \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{45.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}{anchor1298}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{45.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{45.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match0 interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR0 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match0 interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR0 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match1 interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR1 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match1 interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR1 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer0\_match2\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match2 interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR2 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match2 interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR2 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_match3\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match3 interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR3 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match3 interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR3 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 wrap interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from all1 to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 wrap interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when TC count from all1 to 0x0.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match0 interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match1 interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize timer0\_match2\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match2 interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize timer0\_match3\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match3 interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from all1 to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match0 interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match1 interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 & \footnotesize timer0\_match2\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match2 interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize timer0\_match3\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match3 interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 23 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 wrap interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{wdg2}
\section{wdg2}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor1486}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor1487}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor1488}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor1489}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4050800C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor1490}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor1491}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{52.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor1492}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{52.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{52.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508C00}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize resetn\_bt\_uar\-t\_clear & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  write 1'b1 clear soft reset \\
\hline
\end{longtable}


\headinginfo{bt\_timer}
\section{bt\_timer}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.1 TIMER0\_CTL}{anchor1755}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.1 TIMER0\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.1 TIMER0\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 reset, active high, cleared automatically\newline
When one, the Timer Counter and the Prescale Counter are synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer0\_mrr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Reset on MR: the TC will be reset if MR matches it.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_mrs & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, Stop on MR: the TC and PC will be stopped and timer0\_en will be set to 0 if MR matches the TC.\newline
0, Feature disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist\newline
0, tick divider exists \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.2 TIMER0\_TC}{anchor1756}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.2 TIMER0\_TC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.2 TIMER0\_TC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 counter\newline
The 32-bit TC is incremented every PR+1 number of TICKs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.3 TIMER0\_PR}{anchor1757}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.3 TIMER0\_PR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.3 TIMER0\_PR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_pr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer0 prescale register \newline
When the Prescale Counter (below) is equal to this value(total timer0\_pr + 1 cycles), the next clock increments the TC and clears the PC. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.4 TIMER0\_PC}{anchor1758}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.4 TIMER0\_PC}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.4 TIMER0\_PC} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4068100C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_pc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 prescale counter \newline
The 32-bit PC is a counter which is incremented\newline
to the value stored in PR. When the value in PR is reached, the TC\newline
is incremented and the PC is cleared. The PC is observable and\newline
controllable through the bus interface. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.5 TIMER0\_MR}{anchor1759}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.5 TIMER0\_MR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.5 TIMER0\_MR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_mr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  timer0 match register \newline
MR can be enabled through the MRR to reset\newline
the TC, the MRS to stop both the TC and PC, and/or generate an interrupt\newline
every time MR matches the TC \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}{anchor1760}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.6 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value\newline
4'd0: disable tick\newline
4'dN(N great than 0): tick is div(N+1) from rtc clock \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_up & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value update, cleared automatically \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}{anchor1761}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.7 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 wrap interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 wrap interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 match interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer0 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer0\_match\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 match interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize timer0\_wrap\_i\-nt\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 wrap interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.23 TIMER2\_INT\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.23 TIMER2\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40681058}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer2\_match\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer2 match interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer2 match interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer2\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer2 wrap interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer2 wrap interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize timer2\_match\_\-int\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer2 match interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. \newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize timer2\_wrap\_i\-nt\_en\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Timer2 wrap interrupt enable clear\newline
1: Clear Enable. An interrupt is generated when TC count from 0xffffffff to 0x0.\newline
0: Ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize timer2\_match\_\-int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 match interrupt clear, active high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize timer2\_wrap\_i\-nt\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 wrap interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{58.24 TIMER2\_INT\_STATUS}{anchor1778}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{58.24 TIMER2\_INT\_STATUS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{58.24 TIMER2\_INT\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4068105C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer2\_match\_\-int\_raw\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 match interrupt raw status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer2\_match\_\-int\_masked\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 match interrupt masked status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize timer2\_wrap\_i\-nt\_raw\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 wrap interrupt raw status \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer2\_wrap\_i\-nt\_masked\_status & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer2 wrap interrupt masked status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}


\headinginfo{bt\_wdg}
\section{bt\_wdg}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor1779}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor1780}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor1781}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor1782}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4068200C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor1783}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor1784}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{59.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor1785}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{59.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{59.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40682C00}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{bt\_dma}
\section{bt\_dma}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.1 SAR0}{anchor1796}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.1 SAR0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.1 SAR0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize SAR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Current Source Address of DMA transfer. Updated after each source transfer. The SINC field in the CTLx register determines whether the address increments, decrements, or is left unchanged on every source transfer through the block transfer. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.2 DAR0}{anchor1797}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.2 DAR0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.2 DAR0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize DAR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Current Destination address of DMA transfer. Updated after each destination transfer. The DINC field in the CTLx register determines whether the address increments, decrements, or is left unchanged on every destination transfer throughout the block transfer. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.3 LLP0}{anchor1798}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.3 LLP0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.3 LLP0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize LMS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  List Master Select. Identifies the AHB layer/interface where the memory device that stores the next linked list item resides.\newline
This field does not exist if the configuration parameter is not set to NO\_HARDCODE. In this case, the read-back value is always the hardcoded value.\newline
The maximum value of this field that can be read back is DMAH\_NUM\_MASTER\_INT-1.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (LIST\_MASTER\_SELECT\_1): The memory device stores the next linked list item on AHB master 1\newline
0x1 (LIST\_MASTER\_SELECT\_2): The memory device stores the next linked list item on AHB master 2\newline
0x2 (LIST\_MASTER\_SELECT\_3): The memory device stores the next linked list item on AHB master 3\newline
0x3 (LIST\_MASTER\_SELECT\_4): The memory device \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 2 & \footnotesize LOC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Starting Address In Memory of next LLI if block chaining is enabled. Note that the two LSBs of the starting address are not stored because the address is assumed to be aligned to a 32-bit boundary. LLI accesses are always 32-bit accesses (Hsize=2) aligned to 32-bit boundaries and cannot be changed or programmed to anything other than 32-bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.4 CTL0\_L}{anchor1799}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.4 CTL0\_L}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.4 CTL0\_L} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize INT\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Interrupt Enable Bit. If set, then all interrupt-generating sources are enabled. Functions as a global mask bit for all interrupts for the channel; raw* interrupt registers still assert if CTLx.INT\_EN=0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (INTERRUPT\_DISABLE): Interrupt is disabled\newline
0x1 (INTERRUPT\_ENABLE): Interrupt is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 1 & \footnotesize DST\_TR\_WIDTH & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Transfer Width. Mapped to AHB bus hsize. For a non-memory peripheral, typically the peripheral (destination) FIFO width.\newline
This value must be less than or equal to DMAH\_Mk\_HDATA\_WIDTH where k is the AHB layer 1 to 4 where the destination resides. For the decoding of this field, see the "Setting Up Transfers" section in the DW\_ahb\_dmac Databook.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_0): Destination transfer width is 8 bits\newline
0x1 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_1): Destination transfer width is 16 bits\newline
0x2 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_2): Destination transfer width is 32 bits\newline
0x3 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_3): Destination transfer width is 64 bits\newline
0x4 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_4): Destination transfer width is 128 bits\newline
0x5 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_5): Destination transfer width is 256 bits\newline
0x6 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_6): Destination transfer width is 256 bits\newline
0x7 (DST\_TR\_WIDTH\_7): Destination transfer width is 256 bits \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize SRC\_TR\_WIDTH & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Transfer Width. Mapped to AHB bus hsize. For a non-memory peripheral, typically the peripheral (source) FIFO width.\newline
This value must be less than or equl to DMAH\_Mk\_HDATA\_WIDTH, where k is the AHB layer 1 to 4 where the source resides.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_0): Source transfer width is 8 bits\newline
0x1 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_1): Source transfer width is 16 bits\newline
0x2 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_2): Source transfer width is 32 bits\newline
0x3 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_3): Source transfer width is 64 bits\newline
0x4 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_4): Source transfer width is 128 bits\newline
0x5 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_5): Source transfer width is 256 bits\newline
0x6 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_6): Source transfer width is 256 bits\newline
0x7 (SRC\_TR\_WIDTH\_7): Source transfer width is 256 bits \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 7 & \footnotesize DINC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Address Increment. Indicates whether to increment or decrement the destination address on every destination transfer. If your device is writing data to a destination peripheral FIFO with a fixed address, then set this field to \"No Change\".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DINC\_0): Increments the destination address\newline
0x1 (DINC\_1): Decrements the destination address\newline
0x2 (DINC\_2): No change in the destination address\newline
0x3 (DINC\_3): No change in the destination address \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 : 9 & \footnotesize SINC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Address Increment. Indicates whether to increment or decrement the source address on every source transfer. If the device is fetching data from a source peripheral FIFO with a fixed address, then set this field to "No change".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (SINC\_0): Increments the source address\newline
0x1 (SINC\_1): Decrements the source address\newline
0x2 (SINC\_2): No change in the source address\newline
0x3 (SINC\_3): No change in the source address \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 11 & \footnotesize DEST\_MSIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Destination Burst Transaction Length. Number of data items, each of width CTLx.DST\_TR\_WIDTH, to be written to the destination every time a destination burst transaction request is made from either the corresponding hardware or software handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE: This value is not related to the AHB bus master HBURST bus.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DEST\_MSIZE\_0): Number of data items to be transferred is 1\newline
0x1 (DEST\_MSIZE\_1): Number of data items to be transferred is 4\newline
0x2 (DEST\_MSIZE\_2): Number of data items to be transferred is 8\newline
0x3 (DEST\_MSIZE\_3): Number of data items to be transferred is 16\newline
0x4 (DEST\_MSIZE\_4): Number of data items to be transferred is 32\newline
0x5 (DEST\_MSIZE\_5): Number of data items to be transferred is 64\newline
0x6 (DEST\_MSIZE\_6): Number of data items to be transferred is 128\newline
0x7 (DEST\_MSIZE\_7): Number of data items to be transferred is 256 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 : 14 & \footnotesize SRC\_MSIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Source Burst Transaction Length. Number of data items, each of width CTLx.SRC\_TR\_WIDTH, to be read from the source every time a burst transferred request is made from either the corresponding hardware or software handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE: This value is not related to the AHB bus master HBURST bus. For information on the decoding for this field, see the "Setting Up Transfers" section and for more inforamtion about this field, see the "Choosing the Receive Watermark level" section in the DW\_ahb\_dmac Databook.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (SRC\_MSIZE\_0): Number of data items to be transferred is 1\newline
0x1 (SRC\_MSIZE\_1): Number of data items to be transferred is 4\newline
0x2 (SRC\_MSIZE\_2): Number of data items to be transferred is 8\newline
0x3 (SRC\_MSIZE\_3): Number of data items to be transferred is 16\newline
0x4 (SRC\_MSIZE\_4): Number of data items to be transferred is 32\newline
0x5 (SRC\_MSIZE\_5): Number of data items to be transferred is 64\newline
0x6 (SRC\_MSIZE\_6): Number of data items to be transferred is 128\newline
0x7 (SRC\_MSIZE\_7): Number of data items to be transferred is 256 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize SRC\_GATHER\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source gather enable. Gather on the source side is applicable only when the CTLx.SINC bit indicates an incrementing or decrementing address control.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (SRC\_GATHER\_DISABLE): Source gather is disabled\newline
0x1 (SRC\_GATHER\_ENABLE): Source gather is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 & \footnotesize DST\_SCATTER\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination scatter enable. Scatter on the destination side is applicable only when the CTLx.DINC bit indicates an incrementing or decrementing address control.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DST\_SCATTER\_DISABLE): Destination Scatter is disabled\newline
0x1 (DST\_SCATTER\_ENABLE): Destination Scatter is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 22 : 20 & \footnotesize TT\_FC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transfer Type and Flow Control. Flow control can be assigned to the DW\_ahb\_dmac, the source peripheral, or the destination peripheral. For more information on transfer types and flow control, refer to "Setup/Operation of the DW\_ahb\_dmac Transfers".\newline
Dependencies: If the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_FC is set to DMA\_FC\_ONLY, then TT\_FC[2] does not exist and TT\_FC[2] always reads back 0.\newline
If DMAH\_CHx\_FC is set to SRC\_FC\_ONLY, then TT\_FC[2:1] does not exist and TT\_FC[2:1] always reads back 2'b10.\newline
If DMAH\_CHx\_FC is set to DST\_FC\_ONLY, then TT\_FC[2:1] does not exist and TT\_FC[2:1] always reads back 2'b11.\newline
For multi-block transfers using linked list operation, TT\_FC must be constant for all blocks of this multi-block transfer.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (TT\_FC\_0): Transfer type is Memory to Memory and Flow Controller is DW\_ahb\_dmac\newline
0x1 (TT\_FC\_1): Transfer type is Memory to Peripheral and Flow Controller is DW\_ahb\_dmac\newline
0x2 (TT\_FC\_2): Transfer type is Peripheral to Memory and Flow Controller is DW\_ahb\_dmac\newline
0x3 (TT\_FC\_3): Transfer type is Peripheral to Peripheral and Flow Controller is DW\_ahb\_dmac\newline
0x4 (TT\_FC\_4): Transfer type is Peripheral to Memory and Flow Controller is Peripheral\newline
0x5 (TT\_FC\_5): Transfer type is Peripheral to Peripheral and Flow Controller is Source Peripheral\newline
0x6 (TT\_FC\_6): Transfer type is Memory to Peripheral and Flow Controller is Peripheral\newline
0x7 (TT\_FC\_7): Transfer type is Peripheral to Peripheral and Flow Controller is Destination Peripheral \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 : 23 & \footnotesize DMS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Master Select. Identifies the Master Interface layer where the destination device (peripheral or memory) resides. The maximum value of this field that can be read back is DMAH\_NUM\_MASTER\_INT-1.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DMS\_0): Destination device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 1\newline
0x1 (DMS\_1): Destination device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 2\newline
0x2 (DMS\_2): Destination device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 3\newline
0x3 (DMS\_3): Destination device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 4 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 26 : 25 & \footnotesize SMS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Master Select. Identifies the Master Interface layer where the source device (peripheral or memory) resides. The maximum value of this field that can be read back is DMAH\_NUM\_MASTER\_INT-1.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (SMS\_0): Source device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 1\newline
0x1 (SMS\_1): Source device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 2\newline
0x2 (SMS\_2): Source device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 3\newline
0x3 (SMS\_3): Source device (peripheral or memory) is accessed from AHB master 4 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 & \footnotesize LLP\_DST\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block chaining is enabled on the destination side only if LLP\_DST\_EN field is high and LLPx.LOC is non-zero. For more information, see "Block Chaining using Linked Lists".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (LLP\_DST\_DISABLE): Block chaining using Linked List is disabled on the Destination side\newline
0x1 (LLP\_DST\_ENABLE): Block chaining using Linked List is enabled on the Destination side \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize LLP\_SRC\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block chaining is enabled on the source side only if the LLP\_SRC\_EN field is high and LLPx.LOC is non-zero. For more information, see "Block Chaining using Linked Lists".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (LLP\_SRC\_DISABLE): Block chaining using Linked List is disabled on the Source side\newline
0x1 (LLP\_SRC\_ENABLE): Block chaining using Linked List is enabled on the Source side \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 29 & \footnotesize RESERVED0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.5 CTL0\_H}{anchor1800}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.5 CTL0\_H}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.5 CTL0\_H} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A001C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize BLOCK\_TS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block Transfer Size.\newline
When the DW\_ahb\_dmac is the flow controller, the user writes this field before the channel is enabled in order to indicate the block size. The number programmed into BLOCK\_TS indicates the total number of single transactions to perform for every block transfer; a single transaction is mapped to a single AMBA beat.\newline
Width: The width of single transaction is determined by CTLx.SRC\_TR\_WIDTH. For further information on setting this field, refer to "Transfer Operation".\newline
Once the transfer starts, the read-back value is the total number of data items already read from the source peripheral, regardless of what is the flow controller.\newline
When the source or destination peripheral is assigned as the flow controller, then the maximum block size that can be read back saturates at DMAH\_CHx\_MAX\_BLK\_SIZE, but the actual block size can be greater. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 1 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize DONE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Done bit.\newline
If status write-back is enabled, the upper word of the control register, CTLx[63:32], is written to the control register location of the Linked List Item (LLI) in system memory at the end of the block transfer with the done bit set.\newline
Software can poll the LLI CTLx.DONE bit to see when a block transfer is complete. The LLI CTLx.DONE bit should be cleared when the linked lists are set up in memory prior to enabling the channel.\newline
LLI accesses are always 32-bit accesses (Hsize=2) aligned to 32-bit boundaries and cannot be changed or programmed to anything other than 32-bit. For more information, refer to "Multi-Block Transfers".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): DONE bit is deasserted the enb of block transfer\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): SET the DONE bit at the end of block transfer \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 13 & \footnotesize RESERVED0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.6 CFG0\_L}{anchor1801}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.6 CFG0\_L}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.6 CFG0\_L} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0040}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 5 & \footnotesize CH\_PRIOR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Priority. A priority of 7 is the highest priority, and 0 is the lowest. This field must be programmed within the range 0 to DMAH\_NUM\_CHANNELS-1. A programmed value outside this range will cause erroneous behavior.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (CH\_PRIOR\_0): Channel priority is 0\newline
0x1 (CH\_PRIOR\_1): Channel priority is 1\newline
0x2 (CH\_PRIOR\_2): Channel priority is 2\newline
0x3 (CH\_PRIOR\_3): Channel priority is 3\newline
0x4 (CH\_PRIOR\_4): Channel priority is 4\newline
0x5 (CH\_PRIOR\_5): Channel priority is 5\newline
0x6 (CH\_PRIOR\_6): Channel priority is 6\newline
0x7 (CH\_PRIOR\_7): Channel priority is 7 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize CH\_SUSP & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Suspend. Suspends all DMA data transfers from the source until this bit is cleared. There is no guarantee that the current transaction will complete. Can also be used in conjunction with CFGx.FIFO\_EMPTY to cleanly disable a channel without losing any data. For more information, refer to "Disabling a Channel Prior to Transfer Completion".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (NOT\_SUSPENDED): DMA transfer from the source is not suspended\newline
0x1 (SUSPENDED): Suspend DMA transfer from the source \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize FIFO\_EMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel FIFO status. Indicates if there is data left in the channel FIFO. Can be used in conjunction with CFGx.CH\_SUSP to cleanly disable a channel. For more information, refer to "Disabling a Channel Prior to Transfer Completion".\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (NOT\_EMPTY): Channel FIFO is not empty\newline
0x1 (EMPTY): Channel FIFO is empty \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize HS\_SEL\_DST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Destination Software or Hardware Handshaking Select. This register selects which of the handshaking interfaces - hardware or software - is active for destination requests on this channel. If the destination peripheral is memory, then this bit is ignored.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (HARDWARE\_HS): Hardware handshaking interface. Software initiated transaction requests are ignored.\newline
0x1 (SOFTWARE\_HS): Software handshaking interface. Hardware initiated transaction requests are ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize HS\_SEL\_SRC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Source Software or Hardware Handshaking Select. This register selects which of the handshaking interfaces - hardware or software - is active for source requests on this channel. If the source peripheral is memory, then this bit is ignored.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (HARDWARE\_HS): Hardware handshaking interface. Software initiated transaction requests are ignored.\newline
0x1 (SOFTWARE\_HS): Software handshaking interface. Hardware initiated transaction requests are ignored.\newline
Value After Reset: 0x1 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize LOCK\_CH\_L & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Local Level. Indicates the duration over which CFGx.LOCK\_CH applies. This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_LOCK\_EN is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (LOCK\_CH\_L\_0): Over complete DMA transfer\newline
0x1 (LOCK\_CH\_L\_1): Over complete DMA block transfer\newline
0x2 (LOCK\_CH\_L\_2): Over complete DMA transaction\newline
0x3 (LOCK\_CH\_L\_3): Over complete DMA transaction \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 14 & \footnotesize LOCK\_B\_L & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Bus lock level. Indicates the duration over which CFGx.LOCK\_B bit applies. This field does not exist if the parameter DMAH\_CHx\_LOCK\_EN is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (LOCK\_B\_L\_0): Over complete DMA transfer\newline
0x1 (LOCK\_B\_L\_1): Over complete DMA block transfer\newline
0x2 (LOCK\_B\_L\_2): Over complete DMA transaction\newline
0x3 (LOCK\_B\_L\_3): Over complete DMA transaction \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize LOCK\_CH & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Lock Bit. When the channel is granted control of the master bus interface and if the CFGx.LOCK\_CH bit is asserted, then no other channels are granted control of the master bus interface for the duration specified in CFGx.LOCK\_CH\_L. Indicates to the master bus interface arbiter that this channel wants exclusive access to the master bus interface for the duration specified in CFGx.LOCK\_CH\_L. This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_LOCK\_EN is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): Channel lock bit is not enabled\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): Channel lock bit is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize LOCK\_B & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Bus Lock Bit. When active, the AHB bus master signal hlock is asserted for the duration specified in CFGx.LOCK\_B\_L. For more information, refer to "Locked DMA Transfers". This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_LOCK\_EN is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): Bus lock bit is not enabled\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): Bus lock bit is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 & \footnotesize DST\_HS\_POL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (ACTIVE\_HIGH): Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active high\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE\_LOW): Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize SRC\_HS\_POL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Handshaking Interface Polarity.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (ACTIVE\_HIGH): Source Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active high\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE\_LOW): Source Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 29 : 20 & \footnotesize MAX\_ABRST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Maximum AMBA Burst Length. Maximum AMBA burst length that is used for DMA transfers on this channel.\newline
A value of 0 indicates that software is not limiting the maximum AMBA burst length for DMA transfers on this channel.\newline
This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_MABRST is not selected; in this case, the read-back value is always 0, and the maximum AMBA burst length cannot be limited by software. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 & \footnotesize RELOAD\_SRC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Automatic Source Reload. The SARx register can be automatically reloaded from its initial value at the end of every block for multi-block transfers. A new block transfer is then initiated. This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_MULTI\_BLK\_EN is not selected; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLE): Source Reload Disabled\newline
0x1 (ENABLE): Source Reload Enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize RELOAD\_DST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Automatic Destination Reload. The DARx register can be automatically reloaded from its initial value at the end of every block for multi-block transfers. A new block transfer is then initiated. This register does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_MULTI\_BLK\_EN is not selected; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLE): Destination Reload Disabled.\newline
0x1 (ENABLE): Destination Reload Enabled \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.7 CFG0\_H}{anchor1802}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.7 CFG0\_H}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.7 CFG0\_H} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0044}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize FCMODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Flow Control Mode. Determines when source transaction requests are serviced when the Destination Peripheral is the flow controller.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (FCMODE\_0): Source transaction requests are serviced when they occur. Data pre-fetching is enabled\newline
0x1 (FCMODE\_1): Source transaction requests are not serviced until a destination transaction request occurs. In this mode, the amount of data transferred from the source is limited so that it is guaranteed to be transferred to the destination prior to block termination by the destination. Data pre-fetching is disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize FIFO\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  FIFO Mode Select. Determines how much space or data needs to be available in the FIFO before a burst transaction request is serviced.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (FIFO\_MODE\_0): Space/data available for single AHB transfer of the specified transfer width\newline
0x1 (FIFO\_MODE\_1): Data available is greater than or equal to half the FIFO depth for destination transfers and space available is greater than half the fifo depth for source transfers. The exceptions are at the end of a burst transaction request or at the end of a block transfer. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 2 & \footnotesize PROTCTL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Protection Control bits used to drive the AHB HPROT[3:1] bus. The AMBA Specification recommends that the default of HPROT indicates a non-cached, non-buffered, privileged data access. The reset value is used to indicate such an access.\newline
HPROT[0] is tied high because all transfers are data accesses, as there are no opcode fetches.\newline
There is a one-to-one mapping of these register bits to the HPROT[3:1] master interface signals.\newline
Mapping of HPROT bus is as follows:\newline
1'b1 to HPROT[0]\newline
CFGx.PROTCTL[1] to HPROT[1]\newline
CFGx.PROTCTL[2] to HPROT[2]\newline
CFGx.PROTCTL[3] to HPROT[3] \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize DS\_UPD\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Status Update Enable. Destination status information is fetched only from the location pointed to by the DSTATARx register, stored in the DSTATx register and written out to the DSTATx location of the LLI, if DS\_UPD\_EN is high. This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_SATAT\_DST is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): Destination Status Update is disabled\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): Destination Status Update is enabled \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize SS\_UPD\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Status Update Enable. Source status information is fetched only from the location pointed to by the SSTATARx register, stored in the SSTATx register and written out to the SSTATx location of the LLI, if SS\_UPD\_EN is high.\newline
NOTE: This enalbe is applicable only if DMAH\_CHx\_STAT\_SRC is set to True. This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_CHx\_STAT\_SRC is set to False; in this case, the read-back value is always 0.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): Source Status Update is disabled.\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): Source Status Update is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize SRC\_PER & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Hardware Interface..\newline
Assigns a hardware handshaking interface (0 : DMAH\_NUM\_HS\_INT-1) to the source of channel x if the CFGx.HS\_SEL\_SRC field is 0; otherwise, this field is ignored. The channel can then communicate with the source peripheral connected to that interface through the assigned hardware handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE1: For correct DW\_ahb\_dmac operation, only one peripheral (source or destination) should be assigned to the same handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE2: This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_NUM\_HS\_INT is set to 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 : 8 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize DEST\_PER & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination hardware interface.\newline
Assigns a hardware handshaking interface (0 : DMAH\_NUM\_HS\_INT-1) to the destination of channel x if the CFGx.HS\_SEL\_DST field is 0; otherwise, this field is ignored. The channel can then communicate with the destination peripheral connected to that interface through the assigned hardware handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE1: For correct DW\_ahb\_dmac operation, only one peripheral (source or destination) should be assigned to the same handshaking interface.\newline
NOTE2: This field does not exist if the configuration parameter DMAH\_NUM\_HS\_INT is set to 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 12 & \footnotesize RESERVED0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.8 SGR0}

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 20 & \footnotesize SGC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Gather Count. Source contiguous tranfer count between successive gather boundaries. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline

\pdfbookmark[2]{61.9 DSR0}{anchor1804}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.9 DSR0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.9 DSR0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A0050}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 : 0 & \footnotesize DSI & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Scatter Interval. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 20 & \footnotesize DSC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Scatter Count. Destination contiguous transfer count between successive scatter boundaries. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.10 RAW\_TFR}{anchor1805}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.10 RAW\_TFR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline

\pdfbookmark[2]{61.32 CH\_EN}{anchor1827}

\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{61.32 CH\_EN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406A03A0}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CH\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Enable. The ChEnReg.CH\_EN bit is automatically cleared by hardware to disable the channel after the last AMBA transfer of the DMA transfer to the destination has completed. Software can therefore poll this bit to determine when this channel is free for a new DMA transfer.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (DISABLED): Disable the channel\newline
0x1 (ENABLED): Enable the channel \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 1 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize CH\_EN\_WE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel enable register \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 9 & \footnotesize RESERVED0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_ID & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Hardcoded DW\_ahb\_dmac peripheral ID.\newline
Value After Reset: DMAH\_ID\_NUM\newline
Exists: Always \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{61.34 DMA\_TEST}{anchor1829}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{61.34 DMA\_TEST}

\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize TEST\_SLV\_IF & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA Test register\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (NORMAL\_MODE): Puts the AHB slave interface into Normal mode\newline
0x1 (TEST\_MODE): Puts the AHB slave interface into Test mode. In this mode, the readback value of the writable registers always matches the values written \\

\footnotesize 31 : 1 & \footnotesize RESERVED & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
