\headinginfo{bt\_uart}
\section{bt\_uart}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}{anchor1845}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.1 RBR\_THR\_DLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rbr\_thr\_dll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  receiver buffer when read, transmit holding register when write(In fifo mode, the written data will push to tx fifo).\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (low) bit [7:0]. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.2 IER\_DLH}{anchor1846}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.2 IER\_DLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.2 IER\_DLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize erbfi\_dlh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx data available interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 0. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize etbei\_dlh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Transmit Holding Register Empty interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize elsi\_dlh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Rx Line Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 2. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize edssi\_dlh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Modem Status interrupt enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 3. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize dlh\_6\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  reserved;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit [6:4]. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_dlh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DLAB == 0:  Programmable THRE interrupt mode enable;\newline
DLAB == 1:  divisor latch (high) bit 7. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.3 IIR\_FCR}{anchor1847}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.3 IIR\_FCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.3 IIR\_FCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize iid\_0\_fifoe & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 0; Write:  fifo enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize iid\_1\_rfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 1; Write:  rx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize iid\_2\_xfifor & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 2; Write:  tx fifo reset. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize iid\_3\_dmam & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read: interrupt ID bit 3; Write:  dma mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize tet & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx Empty Trigger. 0: fifo empty. 1: 2 characters in the fifo. 2: fifo 1/4 full. 3: fifo 1/2 full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize fifose\_rcvr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx trigger. 0: 1 character in the fifo. 1: fifo 1/4 full. 2: fifo 1/2 full. 3: fifo 2 less than full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.4 LCR}{anchor1848}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.4 LCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.4 LCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B000C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize dls & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data length select. 0: 5bit, 1: 6bit, 2: 7bit, 3:8bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize stop & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of stop bits. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize pen & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize eps & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Even Parity select. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize stick\_parity & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Stick parity. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize bc & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break control bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dlab & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Divisor latch access bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.5 MCR}{anchor1849}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.5 MCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.5 MCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dtr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data terminal ready. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize rts & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request to send. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize out1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT1. This is used to directly control the user-designated Output1 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize out2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  OUT2.  This is used to directly control the user-designated Output2 output. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize lb & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Loopback mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize afce & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Auto flow control enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize sire & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SIR mode enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.6 LSR}{anchor1850}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.6 LSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.6 LSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data ready bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize oe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Overrun error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize pe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Parity Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize fe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Framing Error bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize bi & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Break interrupt bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize thre & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter holding register empty bit. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize temt & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmiter empty bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receiver fifo error bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.7 MSR}{anchor1851}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.7 MSR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.7 MSR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dcts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta clear to send. This is used to indicate that the modem control line cts\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ddsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data set ready. This is used to indicate that the modem control line dsr\_n has changed since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects changes on MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize teri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Trailing edge of ring indicator. This is used to indicate that a change on the input ri\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[2](Out1) has changed state from a high to low. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ddcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Delta data carrier detect. This is used to indicate that a change on line dcd\_n has occurred since the last time the MSR was read. Reading the MSR clears this bit. In loopback mode, this bit reflects when MCR[3](Out1) has changed. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize cts & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Clear to send. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line cts\_n. \newline
0: cts\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: cts\_n input is asserted(logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[1](RTS). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dsr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data set ready. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dsr\_n. \newline
0: dsr\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dsr\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[0](DTR). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize ri & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Ring indicator. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line ri\_n. \newline
0: ri\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: ri\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[2](Out1). \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize dcd & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Carrier Detect. This is used to indicate the current state of the modem control line dcd\_n. \newline
0: dcd\_n input is de-asserted(logic 1)\newline
1: dcd\_n input is asserted (logic 0).\newline
In loopback mode, this bit is the same as MCR[3](Out2). \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.8 SCR}{anchor1852}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.8 SCR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.8 SCR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B001C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize scr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is for programmers to use as a temporary storage space. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.9 LPDLL}{anchor1853}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.9 LPDLL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.9 LPDLL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0020}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdll & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the lower 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.10 LPDLH}{anchor1854}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.10 LPDLH}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.10 LPDLH} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0024}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize lpdlh & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register makes up the upper 8-bits of a 16-bit Low Power Divisor Latch register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART, which must give a baud rate of 115.2K. This is required for SIR Low Power detection at the receiver. This register may only be accessed when the DLAB bit is set and the UART is not busy. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.11 FAR}{anchor1855}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.11 FAR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.11 FAR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0070}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize fa & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  FIFO access regiseter. 1: FIFO access mode is enabled. 0: disabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.12 TFR}{anchor1856}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.12 TFR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.12 TFR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0074}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize tfr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO read. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.13 RFW}{anchor1857}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.13 RFW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.13 RFW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B0078}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfwd & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo write data. These bits are only valid when FIFO access mode is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize rfpe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo parity error. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize rffe & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive fifo framing error. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.14 USR}{anchor1858}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.14 USR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.14 USR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B007C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: uart is idle or inactive; 1: uart is busy. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tfnf & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO not full. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tfe & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Transmit FIFO empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize rfne & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO not empty. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize rff & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Receive FIFO full. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.15 TFL}{anchor1859}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.15 TFL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.16 RFL}{anchor1860}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.16 RFL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize rfl & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This indicates the number of data entries in the receive fifo. \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{62.17 HTX}{anchor1861}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.17 HTX}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.17 HTX} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B00A4}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize htx & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to halt transmissions for testing, so that the transmit fifo can be filled by the master. 0: halt tx disabled; 1: halt tx enabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{62.18 DMASA}{anchor1862}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{62.18 DMASA}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{62.18 DMASA} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x406B00A8}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize dmasa & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This register is use to perform a DMA software acknowledge if a transfer needs to be terminated due to an error condition. For example, if the DMA disables the channel, then the DW\_apb\_uart should clear its request. This cause the tx request, tx single, rx request and rx signle signals to de-assert.  This bit is 'self-clearing' \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{wdg4}
\section{wdg4}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor1979}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001400}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor1980}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001404}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor1981}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001408}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor1982}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x7000140C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor1983}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001410}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor1984}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001414}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{66.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor1985}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{66.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{66.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70002000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
